definitions.en: An exclamation used to express surprise, disappointment, or concern.
definitions.vi: Câu cảm thán dùng để bày tỏ sự ngạc nhiên, thất vọng, hoặc lo lắng.

example.en: Oh my god, I forgot to bring the keys!
example.vi: Trời ơi, tôi quên mang chìa khóa rồi!

exampleSentences: Trời ơi, em quên mang chìa khóa ở nhà rồi!
Trời ơi, sao anh lại làm mất điện thoại nữa vậy?
Trời ơi, hôm nay trời nóng quá nên chị không muốn ra ngoài.

content.sentence: Bạn có ___ khi nghe tin này không?

translation.en: Are you shocked when hearing this news?

notes:
Trời ơi means “oh my God!” or “oh my gosh!” It shows strong emotion — surprise, shock, frustration, excitement, even admiration.

It’s an exclamation, so you just say it at the beginning (or sometimes end) of a sentence to react to something. No grammar changes needed.

Literally, trời = “sky/heaven,” and ơi is a calling particle, like calling out to the sky.

Very close to “Ôi!” (shorter, lighter) and similar in feeling to English “OMG!” but can be used for both good and bad surprises.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Oh my god, I forgot to bring my keys from home!
Oh my god, why did you lose your phone again?
Oh my god, it’s so hot today that I don’t want to go outside.
notes_md:
**Trời ơi** means “oh my God!” or “oh my gosh!” It shows strong emotion — surprise, shock, frustration, excitement, even admiration.

It’s an exclamation, so you just say it at the beginning (or sometimes end) of a sentence to react to something. No grammar changes needed.

Literally, *trời* = “sky/heaven,” and *ơi* is a calling particle, like calling out to the sky.

Very close to **“Ôi!”** (shorter, lighter) and similar in feeling to English “OMG!” but can be used for both good and bad surprises.

content.sentence: Cô ấy đã nói rằng ___ không thể xảy ra.

translation.en: She said that it cannot happen.
